title: Just One Little Taste
lesson: Being brave enough to try a new healthy food can lead to a yummy surprise.
prompts: At a cozy family dinner table in the evening, warm indoor light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits, resting their chin on one hand and eyeing a plate of colourful broccoli and carrots, with a wary, unsure expression. Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} sits with their own plate, lifting a forkful and smiling gently. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. If a prop is shared, show one shared prop linking their hands in one common space. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

gists: At a cozy evening family dinner table, the child rests their chin on a hand eyeing a plate of broccoli and carrots warily while the mother lifts a forkful, smiling gently.
At the dinner table, the child pushes the plate of broccoli away with a frown, reluctant, while the mother leans in watching with patient encouragement.
At the dinner table, the child holds a fork with one piece of broccoli, curious and considering, while the mother gives an encouraging nod — exactly two people. Deciding to try.
At the dinner table, the child sits alone in close view taking a brave first little bite of broccoli, eyes scrunched.
At the dinner table, the child sits alone with hands pressed to their cheeks in delight, eyes wide with a surprised smile — realising the food is delicious.
At the dinner table, the child happily eats from the plate, joyful, while the mother claps, proud — being brave to try was tasty.
texts: At dinner, {NAME} looked at the green broccoli and orange carrots. "Hmm, I'm not so sure about these," said {NAME}. Mum just smiled.
{NAME} pushed the plate away with a frown. "I don't want to," said {NAME}. "That's okay — take your time," said Mum, waiting patiently.
"Just try one little taste," said Mum with a kind nod. "Only one?" asked {NAME}. "Only one," she smiled. {NAME} picked up a fork.
{NAME} scrunched both eyes shut tight and took a tiny, brave bite. "Crunch!" went the broccoli.
Then {NAME}'s eyes popped wide open. "Mmm — it's yummy!" said {NAME}. "What a delicious surprise!"
{NAME} happily ate up every bit. "More, please!" said {NAME}. "So brave!" said Mum, clapping. Being brave was tasty after all.
